content.text: Part 3: The Core Functional Job for DentalTechHub
Job Statement
"Selecting the right technology solution to address a specific practice need"
This job is performed by
multiple executors
(Practice Owner, Office Manager, DSO Executive, DSO Procurement Specialist) but follows a
common process
regardless of who does it.
Job Map: 12 Chronological Steps
Step
Job Step Statement
Define
the operational or clinical problem requiring a technology solution
Determine
the requirements and constraints for an acceptable solution
Identify
the category of technology that addresses the need
Discover
available solutions in the market
Screen
solutions against basic requirements to create a shortlist
Evaluate
shortlisted solutions in depth (features, pricing, reviews, demos)
Validate
solution fit through references, trials, or proof-of-concept
Compare
finalists and make a selection decision
Negotiate
terms, pricing, and contracts with the selected vendor
Approve
the purchase through internal stakeholders or budget holders
Implement
the selected solution into practice operations
Assess
whether the solution delivered expected value post-implementation
Part 4: Desired Outcomes by Job Step
Job Step 1: Define the operational or clinical problem requiring a technology solution
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize ambiguity about what problem needs to be solved
Minimize the likelihood of solving the wrong problem
Minimize time spent debating whether technology is the right answer
Minimize misalignment between stakeholders on the problem definition
Minimize the risk of addressing symptoms rather than root causes
Job Step 2: Determine the requirements and constraints for an acceptable solution
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize requirements that are unrealistic or unnecessary
Minimize overlooked requirements that surface later
Minimize ambiguity about must-haves vs. nice-to-haves
Minimize the effort required to document requirements
Minimize budget constraints discovered late in the process
Minimize technical constraints (integrations, infrastructure) discovered late
Job Step 3: Identify the category of technology that addresses the need
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize confusion about what type of solution to look for
Minimize the risk of searching in the wrong category
Minimize time spent learning unfamiliar technology categories
Minimize overlooking emerging categories that could solve the problem better
Minimize jargon and terminology confusion
Job Step 4: Discover available solutions in the market
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize the time it takes to find relevant solutions
Minimize the likelihood of missing strong contenders
Minimize noise from irrelevant or low-quality options
Minimize reliance on biased sources (vendor marketing, paid ads)
Minimize the effort required to compile an initial list
Minimize outdated information about available solutions
Job Step 5: Screen solutions against basic requirements to create a shortlist
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize time spent evaluating obviously unsuitable options
Minimize the likelihood of eliminating good options prematurely
Minimize the effort required to check basic fit criteria
Minimize inconsistency in how options are screened
Minimize shortlists that are too long or too short
Job Step 6: Evaluate shortlisted solutions in depth (features, pricing, reviews, demos)
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize the time required to deeply evaluate each option
Minimize inaccurate or outdated product information
Minimize difficulty comparing features across solutions
Minimize hidden pricing or cost surprises
Minimize reliance on vendor claims without independent validation
Minimize the effort required to schedule and conduct demos
Minimize fake or untrustworthy reviews
Minimize difficulty finding reviews from similar practices
Job Step 7: Validate solution fit through references, trials, or proof-of-concept
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize the difficulty of finding relevant reference customers
Minimize the time required to conduct a trial or pilot
Minimize the risk of trials that don't reflect real-world use
Minimize vendor friction in providing trial access
Minimize references that are cherry-picked or unrepresentative
Minimize the effort required to evaluate trial results
Job Step 8: Compare finalists and make a selection decision
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize difficulty making apples-to-apples comparisons
Minimize analysis paralysis from too many factors
Minimize decisions based on incomplete information
Minimize regret from choosing the wrong solution
Minimize stakeholder disagreement on the final choice
Minimize the time from evaluation completion to decision
Job Step 9: Negotiate terms, pricing, and contracts with the selected vendor
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize overpaying relative to fair market price
Minimize unfavorable contract terms
Minimize hidden fees discovered after signing
Minimize time spent in negotiation
Minimize lock-in clauses that reduce future flexibility
Minimize the complexity of understanding contract terms
Job Step 10: Approve the purchase through internal stakeholders or budget holders
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize delays in getting purchase approved
Minimize rework to justify the decision to stakeholders
Minimize surprises that derail approval
Minimize the effort required to build a business case
Minimize approvers' concerns about the recommendation
Job Step 11: Implement the selected solution into practice operations
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize the time from purchase to go-live
Minimize disruption to practice operations during implementation
Minimize implementation surprises not anticipated during evaluation
Minimize staff resistance to the new solution
Minimize vendor support gaps during implementation
Minimize training time required for staff proficiency
Job Step 12: Assess whether the solution delivered expected value post-implementation
Desired Outcome Statement
Minimize the gap between expected and actual value delivered
Minimize difficulty measuring solution impact
Minimize time spent troubleshooting underperformance
Minimize switching costs if solution fails to deliver
Minimize the likelihood of repeating mistakes in future selections
Part 5: Related Jobs
Before the Core Job
Related Job Statement
Staying informed about technology trends in dental
Benchmarking current technology stack against peers
Building internal alignment on technology investment priorities
Securing budget for technology investments
During the Core Job
Related Job Statement
Educating oneself on unfamiliar technology categories
Coordinating evaluation activities across stakeholders
Managing vendor communications and follow-ups
Documenting evaluation findings and decisions
After the Core Job
Related Job Statement
Managing ongoing vendor relationship
Tracking solution performance and ROI
Planning for upgrades, renewals, or replacements
Sharing learnings with peers about solution performance
Part 6: Emotional Jobs
Emotional Job Statement
Feel confident that I'm making an informed decision
Feel protected from making a costly mistake
Feel in control of the process despite complexity
Feel smart and capable navigating technology decisions
Feel secure that I won't be blamed if the solution fails
Feel relieved when the decision is made
Feel proud of selecting a solution that works well
Feel trusted by stakeholders for my recommendation
Part 7: Social Jobs
Social Job Statement
Be perceived by leadership as making sound technology decisions
Be perceived by staff as selecting tools that make their jobs easier
Be perceived by peers as technologically savvy
Be perceived by vendors as a knowledgeable, serious buyer
Be perceived as a good steward of practice resources
Be perceived as someone who does thorough due diligence
Part 8: DentalTechHub Value Opportunity Analysis
Highest-Value Job Steps for Marketplace Innovation
Based on the desired outcomes analysis,
DentalTechHub has the highest opportunity to create value in Job Steps 4-8
Job Step
Opportunity Level
Why High Opportunity
Step 4: Discover
🔴 Very High
Fragmented information, biased sources, no central hub
Step 5: Screen
🔴 Very High
Manual process, inconsistent criteria, time-consuming
Step 6: Evaluate
🔴 Very High
Hidden pricing, outdated info, hard to compare, fake reviews
Step 7: Validate
🟠 High
Hard to find references, vendor-controlled trial access
Step 8: Compare
🟠 High
No apples-to-apples tools, analysis paralysis
Lower Opportunity Steps (Marketplace Less Applicable)
Job Step
Opportunity Level
Why Lower
Steps 1-3
🟡 Medium
Problem definition is internal; marketplace can provide education/content
Step 9
🟡 Medium
Negotiation is vendor-buyer relationship; marketplace can provide benchmarks
Steps 10-12
🟢 Lower
Implementation/assessment happen outside marketplace scope
